overview: Port the DopaQiX web game from main into a native Windows Godot build while preserving the web game's rail-cutting rules, reveal loop, pickup behavior, soundtrack feel, and front-end framing closely enough that it reads as the same game, not a reinterpretation.
goals: Match the main-branch gameplay loop: rail-only movement, empty-field cuts, area capture, sparks on rail or trail, QiX in field space, time pressure, scoring, and level progression.
Match the main-branch presentation closely: dark board, candy HUD, grayscale claimed reveal during play, full image reveal on level clear, right-side control panel, and reactive synth music.
Ship a Windows-native Godot export with an exe and zip that a non-technical player can launch directly.
nonGoals: Do not preserve the browser runtime or index.html shipping path.
Do not add a new interpretation-heavy art direction that drifts from the main-branch web game.

stories.title: Port core web rules into Godot
stories.description: Bring over the web game's movement, trail, capture, enemy, spark, pickup, score, and timer logic so the core run feels like the browser source.
acceptanceCriteria: The player can only travel the rail when not drawing and can only start a cut from rail into adjacent empty field.
Sparks stay on rail or active trail paths, QiX stay in field space, and trail contact still costs a life.
Scoring, level goal, burst threshold, and timer all update during active play.

stories.title: Match the main presentation more closely
stories.description: Rework the Godot HUD, sidebar, overlays, reveal rendering, and music so the native game reads as the same product family as the web build.
acceptanceCriteria: The board keeps the dark framed look with grayscale reveal during play and full image reveal on level clear.
The top HUD and right-side panel communicate the same information and controls as the web version.
Music changes with reveal pool and intensifies under danger instead of using a generic background loop.

stories.title: Package and release a player build
stories.description: Export a Windows build, zip it with a short run note, and publish it from the new repo.
acceptanceCriteria: The export script creates a Windows executable and matching win64 zip under dist.
The repo documents the release path and links players to the latest release.
The built artifact is suitable for non-technical players to unzip and run.
